channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Senate advances nomination of Kash Patel, Trump's pick for FBI director
item.description: The Senate voted Tuesday along party lines to advance the nomination of Kash Patel, President Donald Trump's pick for FBI director, setting up a final vote on the Trump ally.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration halts support for representing unaccompanied children in immigration court
item.description: The Trump administration has stopped support for legal representation in immigration court for children who enter the United States alone, a setback for those fighting deportation who can’t afford a lawyer.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A federal judge has denied states‘ bid to halt DOGE and Musk‘s work
item.description: A federal judge has denied a bid to temporarily halt DOGE from its controversial work at certain federal agencies. Meanwhile, the White House says Elon Musk is not technically DOGE’s leader.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler, MPR News

item.title: First openly gay DC federal judge rakes Trump admin over military trans ban
item.description: A federal court on Tuesday heard arguments over whether the Trump administration has attempted to ban transgender service members from the U.S. military.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Watch: Trump signs executive order at Mar-a-Lago expanding IVF access
item.description: "The Order directs policy recommendations to protect IVF access and aggressively reduce out-of-pocket and health plan costs for such treatments," the White House press secretary wrote.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, NewsNation

item.title: Kennedy says panel will examine childhood vaccine schedule after promising not to change it
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — To earn the vote he needed to become the nation's top health official, Robert F. Kennedy Jr. made a special promise to a U.S. senator: He would not change the nation's current vaccination schedule. But on Tuesday, speaking for the first time to thousands of U.S.
item.dc:creator: AMANDA SEITZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump signals 25 percent tariffs coming on cars, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday indicated he would unveil tariffs on imports of automobiles, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals in early April, starting at 25 percent.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia, Ukraine take 'significant first step toward peace' after Rubio-led negotiations, White House insists
item.description: President Donald Trump said Tuesday he believes he can end the conflict between Russia and Ukraine after meetings between U.S. and Russian officials in Saudi Arabia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Postmaster General Louis DeJoy stepping down amid US Postal Service financial turmoil
item.description: The U.S. Postal Service on Tuesday said that Postmaster General Louis DeJoy will be stepping down and that a successor will be named following a search.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge denies Democrat-led effort to block DOGE access, citing lack of proven harm
item.description: A Wasington, D.C.-based federal judge has declined to block billionaire Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) from accessing government data or firing federal employees.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US GM Bill Guerin says he would welcome Trump when Americans play Canada for 4 Nations title
item.description: The general manager of the U.S. team in the 4 Nations Face-Off hockey tournament said he would welcome a visit from President Donald Trump to the championship game between the United States and Canada on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Wisconsin Supreme Court rules Republican had no right to bring lawsuit challenging mobile voting
item.description: A divided Wisconsin Supreme Court ruled Tuesday that a Republican Party official lacked the standing to bring a lawsuit challenging the use of a mobile voting van in 2022.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: New York City Mayor Eric Adams will face judge who will decide whether to dismiss his criminal case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams will face the federal judge on Wednesday who will decide whether to grant the Justice Department's request to dismiss corruption charges against him after lawyers explain the abrupt change in position just weeks before an April trial. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Social Security chief steps down over DOGE access to recipient data: Reports
item.description: The Social Security Administration’s acting leader stepped down from her role over requests from the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) to access recipient data, according to several reports.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Education Department warns schools to cut DEI programs or lose funding
item.description: Twelve states have already passed laws to DEI efforts, while another twelve are considering similar measures.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: House, Senate standoff over Trump agenda escalates
item.description: The showdown between House and Senate Republicans is escalating as the two groups barrel ahead with their contrasting strategies to pass President Trump’s legislative agenda — with the upper chamber expected to move this week.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Bill O'Reilly: 35% of people will never support Trump, DOGE
item.description: "I want this; 80% of the American people are with me. The Trump haters don't want it," O'Reilly said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Trump begins firings of FAA staff just weeks after fatal DC plane crash
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has begun firing several hundred Federal Aviation Administration employees, upending staff on a busy air travel weekend and just weeks after a January fatal midair collision at Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Health agencies lose staff members in key areas as Trump firings set in
item.description: As the dust settles from the first wave of firings at health agencies, here's how many people got cut and the impact of the roles that were lost.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone and Pien Huang, MPR News

item.title: New Florida Attorney General sworn in, first priority illegal immigration
item.description: Florida’s new Attorney General has been sworn in and he's hitting the ground, running. After taking the oath of office, James Uthmeier stood with Gov. Ron DeSantis and the cabinet holding an emergency meeting to implement immigration enforcement measures.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Muller, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FAA firings begin weeks after deadly crash near DC
item.description: The firings come just weeks after an American Airlines flight collided mid-air with a military Black Hawk helicopter shortly before landing at Reagan National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Trump suggests no laws violated if he ‘saves’ country
item.description: Trump doubled down on the Napoleon reference on Sunday, reposting an image of the French emperor riding into battle with the text "He who saves his country violates no law" overlaid.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Trump nominates Ed Martin, an ally who defended Jan. 6 rioters, to stay as top DC federal prosecutor
item.description: Conservative activist Ed Martin, who echoed Donald Trump's baseless claims of a stolen 2020 election and defended Jan. 6, 2021, U.S. Capitol rioters, was nominated by the president on Monday to be the top federal prosecutor in Washington on a permanent basis.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: A fired national park ranger lost his dream job. He says the public is losing more
item.description: Brian Gibbs was an environmental educator at Effigy Mounds National Monument in Iowa. His job was among those nixed by the Trump administration as it pushes to shrink federal spending.
item.dc:creator: Emma Bowman, MPR News

item.title: Trump names Jim Byron to senior archivist role
item.description: “Jim will manage the National Archives on a day-to-day basis, while we continue our search for a full-time Archivist,” Trump posted.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu signals he's moving ahead with Trump's idea to transfer Palestinians from Gaza
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Sunday signaled that he was moving ahead with U.S. President Donald Trump's proposal to transfer the Palestinian population out of Gaza, calling it “the only viable plan to enable a different future" for the region.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The latest: Federal workers face mass layoffs
item.description: Federal workers all over the country have responded with anger and confusion Friday toward President Donald Trump’s aggressive effort to shrink the size of the federal workforce.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Florida passes immigration bill, repealing in-state tuition for undocumented students
item.description: The third and final day of the special session concluded with the passage of an immigration bill, one that sparked significant debate among lawmakers in both the House and Senate.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada joins lawsuit challenging Elon Musk's executive power
item.description: Democratic Nevada Attorney General Aaron Ford joined more than a dozen other attorneys general Thursday in filing a lawsuit challenging Elon Musk's executive power.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News
